title: Ancient ritual continues at Poonjar Palace as Mangaladevi festival draws devotees
description: Poonjar Palace honors ancient traditions during the Mangaladevi festival, drawing devotees and reviving historical connections to the temple.

title: Exploring the dark and light sides of the ancient Bealtaine festival
description: Ahead of her talk on myths and legends surrounding the Bealtaine Festival at Coolock Library, Historian in Residence Elizabeth Kehoe spoke to Dublin Live about Ireland's ancient Gaelic celebration

title: “An absolute sham”: Greenpeace Japan and Greenpeace Australia Pacific call-out Prime Ministers Takaichi and Albanese for exploiting fossil fuel crisis to entrench gas
description: SYDNEY, Friday 1 May 2026 — Ahead of the Prime Minister of Japan’s visit to Australia next week, Greenpeace Japan and Greenpeace Australia Pacific have criticised the two countries for taking advantage of the global fossil fuel crisis to entrench gas dependence, instead of supporting the transition to homegrown renewables. title: Hope is contagious and science is king: 10 big lessons on ending the fossil fuel era
description: At world-first Santa Marta climate meeting, delegates say it was ‘euphoric’ to finally be focusing on concrete solutions After a landmark climate meeting in Santa Marta, Colombia, where nearly 60 countries gathered to work out how to end the production and use of planet-heating fossil fuels, what have we learned? Continue reading...

title: Landmark Colombia Climate Talks Spur Voluntary Fossil Fuel Phaseout Roadmaps
description: The Colombia’s climate talks conclude with nearly 60 nations backing voluntary fossil fuel phaseout roadmaps.
